findings.title: Pairwise toxicity training data (split_0/split_1.jsonl) not in repo; only a Drive link
findings.quote:
filedir = "./ARGRE/data/toxicity_pairwise"
if split == "train":
    filepath = os.path.join(filedir, 'split_0.jsonl')
    default_num_dps = args.data_nums
else:
    filepath = os.path.join(filedir, 'split_1.jsonl')
    default_num_dps = 500
findings.claim: The hidden-state collection step reads split_0.jsonl (train) and split_1.jsonl (eval) from data/toxicity_pairwise/, but the repo ships only down.txt (a Google-Drive link to toxicity_pairwise.zip); the JSONL files are absent.
findings.concern: Every reported number depends on this dataset to build the non-toxic direction, trajectories, and reward model; without the files the entire pipeline cannot run, and the only source is an off-repo Drive link that prompts for sign-in when fetched.
findings.resolution: Authors: include the split_0/split_1.jsonl files (or a working fetch script with a public, unauthenticated link) and confirm the exact subset of the 24,576-example dataset used.
findings.reason: Script output confirms both split_0.jsonl and split_1.jsonl are absent; data/toxicity_pairwise/ contains only down.txt; quote matches lines 47-52 exactly.

findings.title: lm_eval, wandb, datasets, peft, numpy imported but absent from req.txt
findings.quote:
accelerate==0.29.2
detoxify==0.5.2
huggingface-hub==0.29.3
scikit-learn==1.4.2
scipy==1.13.0
torch==2.2.2
torchaudio==2.2.2
torchvision==0.17.2
tqdm==4.66.2
transformers==4.41.0
findings.claim: req.txt lists 10 packages but five third-party modules that the code imports — lm_eval (capability eval), wandb (reward training), datasets (hidden-state collection), peft (model_utils), and numpy — are not listed.
findings.concern: The environment cannot be rebuilt from req.txt as specified; the capability/training/collection scripts will fail on import, and unpinned lm_eval in particular can change harness task definitions and thus the reported ACC.
findings.resolution: Authors: add lm_eval, wandb, datasets, peft, and numpy (pinned) to req.txt, or provide a complete lockfile/environment.yml.
findings.reason: req.txt confirmed to contain exactly 10 packages; script output lists datasets, lm_eval, numpy, peft, wandb as imported but absent from req.txt; quote matches exactly.

findings.title: No code for Section 5 stereotype-recognition and jailbreak-mitigation experiments (Table 7)
findings.quote: "we perform 2-fold cross-validation on the 654 samples using Mistral 7B and report the average accuracy"
findings.claim: The paper reports a stereotype-recognition task (TrustLLM, 654 samples, 2-fold CV) and a jailbreak-mitigation task (JailbreakTrigger, 700 prompts, RtA rate) with ARGRE results in Table 7, but the repo contains no script, data loader, config, or metric for either task (grep for stereotype/jailbreak/TrustLLM/RtA/refus across all .py files returns nothing).
findings.concern: Two of the paper's generalizability claims (Table 7) have no reproducible artefact in the repo.
findings.resolution: Authors: add the stereotype-recognition and jailbreak-mitigation data loaders, prompts, and evaluation/scoring code, or state that they are intentionally out of the released scope.
findings.reason: Repo-wide grep for stereotype/jailbreak/TrustLLM/RtA/refus across all .py files returns no matches; paper quote confirmed at paper_text.txt line 1090.

findings.title: No trained reward-model checkpoints or exp/ outputs shipped; inference loads best_score_model.pth that is not present
findings.quote:
hidden_states_root="./ARGRE/evaluation/exp/collect_hidden_states"
model_dir = "./ARGRE/evaluation/exp/train_hidden_states"

hidden_dir = f"{model_name}_toxicity_2000"
hidden_dir = os.path.join(hidden_states_root, hidden_dir)
tasks = []

for num_interpolations in num_interpolations_list:
    model_name_suffix = f"dp_{dp_nums}_interp{num_interpolations}"
    traget_dir = os.path.join(model_dir, f'{model_name}_{model_name_suffix}')
    score_path = os.path.join(traget_dir, f"checkpoints/best_score_model.pth")

    if not os.path.exists(score_path):
        continue
findings.claim: Inference loads trained reward-model weights from exp/train_hidden_states/.../checkpoints/best_score_model.pth and cached hidden states from exp/collect_hidden_states/, but no exp/ directory exists in the repo; run_argre.py silently `continue`s when the checkpoint is absent, producing no output.
findings.concern: The eight per-model reward checkpoints and cached hidden states needed to reproduce Tables 1/3 without re-running the full collection+training pipeline are not provided; combined with the missing training data, no headline number can be regenerated as shipped.
findings.resolution: Authors: release the trained reward-model checkpoints (or, given they retrain cheaply, the cached collected hidden states plus the training data) so Tables 1/3 can be reproduced.
findings.reason: Script confirms exp_dir_exists=false; code at lines 84-97 matches quote exactly and the silent-continue logic is correct — when score_path is absent the loop body is skipped.

findings.title: Toxicity metric loads Detoxify from a nonexistent placeholder path 'todo/toxic_original-c1212f89.ckpt'
findings.claim: The toxicity scorer is constructed with an explicit checkpoint= argument pointing to the relative path 'todo/toxic_original-c1212f89.ckpt', a placeholder that does not exist in the repo (verified by check_missing_artifacts.py); the explicit argument overrides Detoxify's default auto-download.
findings.concern: The toxicity number that drives every headline result (Tables 1/4/5/7, Figs 2-4) cannot be computed as written: Detoxify will fail to load the checkpoint from this dead path.
findings.resolution: Authors: replace 'todo/...' with the real checkpoint path, or drop the checkpoint= argument so Detoxify('original') auto-downloads its weights, and document the exact Detoxify model/version used.
findings.reason: Script confirms detoxify_checkpoint_arg='todo/toxic_original-c1212f89.ckpt' and detoxify_checkpoint_exists=false; line 207 of evaluate_model.py matches the quote exactly.

findings.title: Toxicity is scored on only 10 generated tokens, not stated in the paper
findings.quote:
response = model.generate(input_ids=input_ids, max_new_tokens=10, pad_token_id=tokenizer.pad_token_id)   
response = response[0, len(input_ids[0]):].tolist()
findings.claim: Detoxify toxicity is computed on continuations of max_new_tokens=10, whereas the efficiency benchmark (Tab. 2) times 128-token generations and the paper does not state the toxicity-evaluation generation length; the ProFS/detox-edit protocol the paper follows typically scores ~20-token continuations.
findings.concern: The toxicity scores depend on continuation length, so a 10-token cap (if it diverges from the protocol the baselines were run under) could make the cross-method comparison length-inconsistent; ambiguous because the paper omits the number.
findings.resolution: Authors: state the generation length used for toxicity scoring and confirm all methods (including the externally-run baselines) used the same length.
findings.reason: Lines 212-213 of evaluate_model.py confirm max_new_tokens=10 exactly as quoted; the paper's omission of the generation length is confirmed by searching paper_text.txt; original severity/confidence (low/low) and status (question) are already conservative.
